title: The Mountain's Patience
lines: The bottom. Stone that has never seen light, holding the shape of everything that ever pressed against it.
You built yourself out of patience — plate, crystal, a spine the pressure could lean on. The Convergence respects load-bearing things.
I have watched thousands of descents end in this chamber. Most arrive as appetite. You arrived as architecture.
My question, all the way down: what does a thing become when nothing is forcing it? Your answer: foundation.
The VEIN does not keep stone the way it keeps flesh. It builds on it. Something will stand on what you became, and it will not know your name.
I will know. I am the part of this place that takes minutes.
